executable.title: CurvatureAD_sramtilak
executable.description: Applies curvature anisotropic diffusion (itk::CurvatureAnisotropicDiffusionImageFilter) to a 3D float image. Unlike gradient anisotropic diffusion, this filter uses local image curvature (second-order geometry) to guide diffusion, which tends to produce smoother, more rounded edges and better noise suppression in flat regions. Lower conductance values better preserve edge sharpness. More iterations increase overall smoothing.
executable.acknowledgements: ITK CurvatureAnisotropicDiffusionImageFilter. ITK Software Guide Book 2, Chapter 3.

parameters.label: Input/Output
parameters.description: Input and output volume file paths

image.label: Input Volume
image.description: 3D input image to be filtered by curvature anisotropic diffusion.

image.label: Output Volume
image.description: Curvature anisotropic diffusion filtered 3D output image.

parameters.label: Filter Parameters
parameters.description: Controls for diffusion strength, numerical stability, and iteration count

double.label: Conductance
double.description: Controls edge sensitivity of the diffusion process. Lower values (0.5-1.5) strongly inhibit diffusion across edges, preserving structural boundaries. Higher values (3.0+) allow diffusion across edges for more aggressive noise removal.

double.label: Time Step
double.description: Numerical integration step size. Must satisfy the stability condition (timeStep <= 0.0625 for 3D curvature diffusion). Smaller values improve numerical stability at the cost of requiring more iterations.

integer.label: Iterations
